skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
(x − 63)² = 4

What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?
rationale: Taking the square root of both sides of (x − 63)² = 4 yields x − 63 = 2 or x − 63 = −2, so x = 65 or x = 61. The sum of the solutions is 65 + 61 = 126.
